title: Isodon glaucocalyx
intro: Isodon glaucocalyx, commonly known as the white Isodon or blue leaf Isodon, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to China and other parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as coughs, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Isodon glaucocalyx include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
